resources.string: Search, open saved pages, and continue from your most visited sites.
This is a local Easy Browser page. Search, suggestions, privacy stats, and Quick Access shortcuts stay inside the browser until you open a web page.
Your most visited pages appear here automatically.
Show protection counters on the home screen.
Show most visited sites on the home screen.
Show current weather and forecast access on the home screen.
Choose which home and new-tab cards appear, then open weather or New Tab Page settings directly.
Current weather, hourly conditions, AQI, and 7-day forecast.
Allow precise location to update weather for your current place.
Used for the home weather widget.
Weather data by MET Norway and Open-Meteo AQI
Account sign-in is not available in this build. Browser data stays local on this device.
Manage Android notification permission and download alerts.
Choose Easy Browser as the default browser in Android settings.
Easy Browser can open http and https links and text shared from other apps. Set it as your default browser in Android settings to route links here automatically.
View open tabs, private tabs, and tab groups.
Choose what opens when Easy Browser starts.
Tabs restored after the last crash
Default layout for the tab manager.
Keep everyday controls visible. Turn on Advanced for developer, DNS, user-agent, user-style, and auto-clear options.
Startup and memory metrics for release checks.
Rules for using Easy Browser responsibly.
How Easy Browser handles data, permissions, and deletion.
How intellectual property concerns are handled.
How app data practices are reviewed and disclosed.
Reject common cookie prompts where supported.
Remove known tracking query parameters from links.
Block unwanted new windows without blocking links you tap.
Store visited pages for history and quick access.
Open website pop-out links in a new browser tab.
Block screenshots and recent-app previews in EasyBrowser.
%1$d of %2$d app permissions granted. Requests only permissions declared by this app.
Debug browser content using external tools
Clear browsing history, cache, and cookies
Request websites not to track your browsing
Camera access is needed to take pictures
Location access is needed for maps and local results
Microphone access is needed for voice search
Storage access is needed to download files
Could not connect to the server
Open in new tab in group
Voice search is not available on this device
This page is using a secure connection. Your information (such as passwords or credit card numbers) is private when it is sent to this website.
Describe the issue or suggestion here.
Block ads, trackers, and heavy scripts.
Protect against CDN tracking where supported.
No app can open this link
This will delete all browsing data, bookmarks, and settings. This cannot be undone.
This will delete your browsing history. This cannot be undone.
This will delete all cookies. You may be signed out of websites.
This will clear the browser cache to free up space.
Storage permission is needed to download files
Browser updates and new feature announcements
This will delete all your browsing history permanently.
No downloads yet. Downloaded files will appear here.
Your browsing history will appear here as you visit websites.
Could not prepare page for printing
Choose where downloaded files are saved
View and clear cookies for specific sites
No sites with stored cookies found in history
Unlock this tab before closing it
Picture-in-Picture not supported on this device
Automatically upgrade insecure http:// requests to https://
Queue downloads when on a metered connection
URL must contain %%s and start with http:// or https://
File not found. Open online instead?
This page is not using a secure connection.
This page has a certificate security problem.
Cookie banner blocking: %1$s. Use the controls below to clear cookies, cache, storage, or permissions for this host.
No saved permissions for this site.
Clear selected data when you close the browser
Default behavior for websites that have not been granted a site-specific permission.
Use desktop user agent and viewport for new browsing sessions.
Controls when inactive tabs are marked for easier cleanup.
Choose when tabs and tab groups are automatically moved to the inactive section.
All duplicate tabs will now also be archived, with the exception of the most recently used copy.
Inactive tabs and groups will be closed after 3 months.
Show Undo Button When Tabs Are Closed
Show a snackbar that can restore a recently closed tab.
Only Open Links in Current Tab Group
New tabs opened from a grouped tab stay in that group.
Use digital rights management to view protected media content in the browser.
Allow audio to continue when you switch tabs or apps, or when the screen is off.
Show the bottom toolbar in browser tabs.
Use dark theme when your device dark theme is on.
Use the active wallpaper as the source for accent colors where supported.
Material You uses Android wallpaper colors when available, with Easy Browser palette fallbacks on older devices.
Rotate only selected favorite wallpapers on the home screen.
Keep the active wallpaper pack in the device cache for smoother offline home loading.
Control cards and shortcuts shown on the home and new tab pages.
Make text and images larger or smaller for all sites that you visit.
Override a website request to prevent zooming in.
Show reader mode when an article can be displayed in a simplified view.
Let websites know the languages that you speak. They can show content in those languages when possible.
Choose one action to show as a shortcut next to the address bar.
You can also change the address bar position from here.
Choose which browser notifications Easy Browser should create, and open Android notification settings for channel-level controls.
Open system notification controls for Easy Browser.
Show completed and failed download notifications.
Show security alerts and tracker reports.
Show forecast and weather warning notifications.
Show daily rewards and achievements when available.
Show AI results and task notifications when available.
Show new feature and browser update notifications.
Download location, behavior, limits, and download notifications.
Open completed downloads with a matching Android app.
Limit download speed for new and resumed downloads.
Use these numbers as a repeatable local baseline before comparing Easy Browser with Brave or Firefox on the same device, network, brightness, and tab set.
Re-read live memory and uptime values.
For store claims, capture the same startup, idle memory, and browsing-flow measurements for Easy Browser, Brave, and Firefox on the same device.
Control tracker blocking, cookie-banner handling, secure navigation, and local privacy protections.
Choose how aggressively Easy Browser blocks trackers and ads.
Unused or duplicate tabs and groups
Tabs and groups unused for %d days
Duplicate tabs and groups or those unused for %d days are moved here. You can change this at any time in settings.
Tabs and groups unused for %d days are moved here. You can change this at any time in settings.
Duplicate tabs and groups are shown here. You can change inactive tab timing in settings.
Choose when inactive tabs should be moved here in settings.
Easy Browser version %1$s. This local in-app document is informational and should be reviewed before each release.

These Terms of Use apply to Easy Browser for Android. By using the app, you agree to use it lawfully, responsibly, and in a way that respects websites, networks, creators, service providers, and other users.

Easy Browser provides browsing with GeckoView, tabs and tab groups, private tabs, bookmarks, history, quick access, reading list, downloads, page sharing, printing, PDF saving, translation links, user styles, extensions, site permissions, cookie controls, and privacy settings.

This build does not provide an Easy Browser sign-in account. Most app records are kept on your device. Websites, search engines, extensions, and Android services may still use their own accounts and terms.
The app, features, settings, dependencies, and these terms may change in future versions. Continued use after an update means you accept the updated app behavior and terms.

Do not use Easy Browser to break laws, abuse network services, bypass access controls, attack systems, distribute harmful software, harass others, misrepresent identity, or interfere with websites or devices.

You choose the URLs, searches, downloads, uploads, extensions, user styles, shortcuts, and shared links you use. You are responsible for checking that your activity is allowed by law and by the relevant service terms.

Only download, save, upload, print, share, or open files that you have the right to use. Completed downloads and saved PDFs may be written to Android Downloads and may be visible to other apps with file access.

Do not use Easy Browser to copy, distribute, stream, modify, or make available copyrighted works, trademarks, software, images, videos, or other protected material without permission or another lawful basis.

Websites, web apps, downloads, cookies, forms, payments, media, and online content are controlled by their owners. Easy Browser does not control their content, security, availability, data practices, or terms.

Search queries and suggestions may be sent to your selected search provider. Translation opens Google Translate with the page URL. DNS-over-HTTPS, when enabled, sends DNS queries to the provider you choose.

Extensions may request permission to read or change website data. User styles inject CSS into matching hosts. Install and create only trusted extensions and styles because they can affect security, privacy, display, and website behavior.

Sharing, printing, file picking, opening downloaded files, adding home-screen shortcuts, notifications, and app settings may involve Android system components or other apps. Those services have their own behavior and policies.

HTTPS-only mode, tracking protection, popup blocking, cookie banner handling, screenshot protection, and private tabs reduce risk but cannot guarantee that every threat, tracker, insecure page, or unsafe download will be blocked.

Easy Browser is provided as is. To the maximum extent permitted by law, compatibility with every website, uninterrupted service, error-free operation, complete security, and fitness for a particular purpose are not guaranteed.
Use Help and Feedback or the developer contact shown on the app store listing for questions about these terms.

This Privacy Policy explains how Easy Browser handles data based on the current codebase. It covers local browser records, permissions, website data, optional analytics and diagnostics, third-party services, retention, and deletion controls.

The local Room database may store history URLs and titles, visit time and count, bookmarks and folders, quick access sites, reading list entries, regular tab URLs and titles, tab groups, favicons, thumbnail paths, and saved Gecko session state. History can be turned off, and private tabs are not saved through the tab repository.

Download records may include URL, file name, MIME type, destination path or MediaStore URI, progress, size, status, error text, speed, remaining time, and timestamps. Reading list and print/PDF features may create local PDF files. Completed downloads are published to Android Downloads.

Preferences may store homepage, search engine, custom search engines, text size, ad blocking level, cookie banner setting, HTTPS-only setting, Do Not Track and Global Privacy Control, site permission defaults, saved host permissions, saved zoom by host, DNS-over-HTTPS provider or URI, user agent settings, language tags, user CSS by host, privacy counters, toolbar/menu settings, download limits, and auto-clear choices.

The app and browser engine may use cache, cookies, DOM storage, authentication sessions, temporary upload copies, temporary print PDFs, download cache files, favicons, thumbnails, and extension storage. Stale upload and download cache files are cleaned by app logic or by clearing cache/app data.

When you open a page, that website and its network providers receive normal browser requests, which can include your IP address, user agent mode or custom user agent, cookies, form entries, uploaded files you choose, and other data you provide to the site.

Address-bar searches are sent to your selected search engine. Suggestions are requested from the matching provider where supported: DuckDuckGo, Google, Bing, Brave Search, Ecosia, Yahoo, or another configured provider. The generated new-tab page uses DuckDuckGo suggestions by default.

The Extensions screen requests extension listings and icons from addons.mozilla.org, and extension installs load XPI files from their source URL. Installed extensions run under GeckoView and may collect or access data according to the permissions you approve and the extension developer policy.

If DNS-over-HTTPS is enabled, DNS queries are sent to Cloudflare, Google, NextDNS, or a custom provider selected in settings. Translate opens translate.google.com with the current page URL so Google can fetch or translate that page.

Share, print, file picker, opening downloaded files, notifications, MediaStore downloads, home-screen shortcuts, and app settings are handled with Android system APIs and may pass data to another app or system service when you choose that action.

This project includes Firebase Analytics and can include Crashlytics when google-services.json is present. App code logs sanitized events: navigation input type, page type and scheme, download MIME group, setting name and on or off state, and extension install source and result. Full URLs and search text are not logged by these app events, and private navigation analytics are skipped.

The manifest declares internet, network state, Wi-Fi state, fine and coarse location, camera, microphone, post notifications, and legacy external storage write permission for Android 9 and below. Camera, microphone, location, notifications, local network, protected media, autoplay, and related website permissions are requested or applied only when a feature or website needs them.

When a website asks for file input, Easy Browser opens Android file picker. Files you choose are copied into app cache as temporary upload files and then handed to GeckoView for the website request. Files you do not choose are not uploaded by this flow.

Allowed site permissions are stored as host and permission type in app preferences. You can revoke saved permissions in Site Settings, and the app asks GeckoView to clear matching browser permission data for that host.

Private tabs start on about:blank, are marked private in GeckoView, are skipped by history recording, are skipped by regular tab persistence, skip navigation analytics in app code, and block copying links to the Android clipboard from private tabs.

Private mode does not hide your activity from websites, network operators, search providers, DNS providers, extensions you allow, downloaded files, screenshots if screenshot protection is off, or data you intentionally share or upload.

The manifest disables Android backup, and backup and data-extraction rules exclude databases, shared preferences, files, cache, external app data, and device-transfer data where the platform honors those rules.

Local browser data remains until you delete it, clear browsing data, remove individual records, use auto-clear on exit, clear app storage in Android settings, or uninstall the app. Some files saved to public Downloads remain outside app storage until you delete them from Android.

Settings can clear history, cookies, cache, DOM storage, authentication sessions, and app cache. Cookie Manager clears cookies by host. Site Info can clear cookies, cache, storage, and permissions for a host. Auto-clear on exit can clear selected data. Bookmarks, downloads, reading list entries, tabs, and app files can also be removed through their screens or Android storage controls.

Easy Browser app code does not sell browser data. Data can still be shared with websites you visit, providers you select, extension services you install, Android services you invoke, Firebase analytics and diagnostics when configured, and other apps when you choose share, open, print, or file actions.
Use Help and Feedback or the developer contact shown on the app store listing to ask privacy questions or request deletion guidance.

Easy Browser respects intellectual property rights and expects users, extension developers, and content providers to do the same. This page explains how IP concerns apply to browsing, downloads, uploads, extensions, user styles, app assets, and reports.
Only access, copy, download, upload, save, print, stream, translate, share, or modify content when you own it, have permission, rely on a lawful exception, or are otherwise authorized.
Do not use Easy Browser to distribute copyrighted files, trademarks, software, images, videos, music, books, databases, designs, or other protected material without authorization.
Do not use the app, extensions, downloads, user styles, developer settings, or custom user agents to bypass access controls, DRM, paywalls, anti-abuse systems, or copyright protection measures.
Downloading a file, saving a page as PDF, printing, uploading selected files, or sharing a link does not grant rights to that content. You remain responsible for how you use or redistribute it.

Easy Browser is a browser and normally does not host or control websites you visit. Reports about web pages, downloads, streams, or marketplace extension content may also need to be sent to the website, host, extension developer, platform, or rights owner process that controls that content.

Only install extensions and create user styles that you are allowed to use. Do not use them to copy protected data, remove rights notices, alter brand presentation in a confusing way, or automate unauthorized access to content.

App icons, screenshots, names, text, source code, bundled assets, and store listing material should be original, licensed, or otherwise authorized before publishing. Keep proof of rights for third-party assets used in the app or listing.

Send notices through Help and Feedback or the developer contact shown on the app store listing. Include the affected URL or app material, a description of the protected work, proof that you can act for the rights owner, contact information, and why the use is unauthorized.

If the concern is a website, download, stream, or extension controlled by another service, the fastest path is usually that service owner or host. If the concern is Easy Browser app code, app assets, or store listing material, contact the app developer.

Reports are reviewed based on the information provided. Where Easy Browser controls the reported material or feature, access may be removed, restricted, corrected, replaced, or clarified when appropriate.
If you believe a report was sent by mistake, use the same contact channel with the affected URL or material, the action taken, and details showing why the use is authorized.

This page summarizes the data and policy controls discovered in the Easy Browser codebase. It is not a legal certification and does not by itself satisfy every law, store policy, or security standard. Review disclosures before every release.

Tracked local data includes Room tables for bookmarks, history, quick access, downloads, reading list, regular tabs, and tab groups; preferences for browser settings, site permissions, search engines, user styles, DNS provider, zoom, user agent, privacy counters, auto-clear, and UI customization; and app files for cache, uploads, downloads, PDFs, favicons, and thumbnails.

Key libraries include GeckoView for browsing, Firebase Analytics and optional Crashlytics, OkHttp for app network calls, Glide for image loading, Room for local storage, Gson and org.json for JSON parsing, Material and AndroidX UI libraries, and Android platform services such as MediaStore, FileProvider, print, notifications, and shortcuts.

Network destinations can include websites opened by the user, selected search and suggestion providers, DuckDuckGo suggestions on the generated new-tab page, addons.mozilla.org marketplace and icons, extension install URLs, DNS-over-HTTPS providers, Google Translate, download URLs, Firebase services when configured, and browser-engine services used by GeckoView.

Core browser records are stored on the device by default. Android backup is disabled and backup rules exclude app databases, preferences, files, cache, and external app data to reduce unintended cloud copies.

Sensitive permissions are tied to browser features and website requests. Location, camera, microphone, notification, local network, protected media, and other site permissions are governed by default settings and per-site prompts or saved host decisions.

Controls include HTTPS-only navigation upgrade, blocking of unsafe data or javascript navigation when HTTPS-only is on, tracker and ad blocking lists, safe browsing via GeckoView settings, cookie banner handling, query-parameter stripping, popup blocking, screenshot protection, path and filename sanitization for downloads, blocked HTTPS-to-HTTP download downgrade redirects, cache cleanup, and user data clearing.

Private tabs use GeckoView private mode, are skipped by local history recording, are skipped by regular tab persistence, skip app navigation analytics, and block link copying to the Android clipboard. Private mode is a local privacy feature, not anonymity from websites or networks.

Privacy Policy and in-app disclosures should cover local browser records, cookies and storage, downloads and saved files, file uploads, search and suggestion providers, extensions, DNS-over-HTTPS, translation, Firebase analytics and diagnostics, Android sharing and file APIs, permissions, retention, deletion, and private-mode limits.

Before Play release, verify the Data Safety form against actual behavior for every build flavor, including Firebase, Crashlytics, permissions, SDKs, extension marketplace calls, search suggestions, file handling, and whether any data is collected, shared, encrypted in transit, optional, or deletable.

Users can clear history, cookies, cache, DOM storage, authentication sessions, host permissions, and app cache from settings; clear cookies and site data by host; delete bookmarks, downloads, reading list entries, tabs, and groups; enable auto-clear on exit; or remove app data/uninstall from Android settings. Files published to public Downloads may need separate deletion.

For each release, review manifest permissions, new SDKs, new network endpoints, new databases or preference keys, new file locations, logging and analytics events, private-mode behavior, backup rules, user deletion paths, screenshots and store listing assets, and support contact details.

Legal and regional obligations vary by distribution channel, user location, age range, data use, SDK behavior, and third-party services. Developer review and, where needed, legal review should be completed before publishing or changing privacy-sensitive features.
